1â†’# Changelog
 2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

22â†’
23â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
24â†’
25â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
26â†’
27â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
28â†’
29â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
30â†’
31â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
32â†’
33â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
34â†’
35â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
36â†’
37â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
38â†’
39â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
40â†’
41â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
42â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
43â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
44â†’
45â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
46â†’
47â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
48â†’
49â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
50â†’

## Summary

I've successfully implemented a fix for the issue "No spacing around colon in annotation". The changes ensure that spacing is not allowed around the colon in annotation and annotation entries regardless of the annotation target.

### Changes Made

#### 1. Modified `SpacingAroundColonRule.kt` (lines 38-62)

**Previous behavior:**
- The rule had a TODO comment and simply returned when encountering colons in annotations, meaning no enforcement of spacing rules.

**New behavior:**
- Implemented active enforcement of no spacing around colons in annotation use-site targets
- Checks for and reports errors on:
  - Whitespace before the colon (except newlines)
  - Whitespace after the colon (except newlines)
- Provides auto-correction by removing the offending whitespace

#### 2. Added comprehensive test case in `SpacingAroundColonRuleTest.kt` (lines 105-149)

**Test coverage includes:**
- `@file :JvmName("Foo")` - space before colon âťŚ
- `@file: JvmName("Foo")` - space after colon âťŚ
- `@file : JvmName("Foo")` - spaces on both sides âťŚ
- `@field :Ann`, `@get: Ann` - property annotation targets âťŚ
- `@set :[Inject VisibleForTesting]`, `@set: [Inject VisibleForTesting]`, `@set : [Inject VisibleForTesting]` - setter annotation targets âťŚ
- `@receiver :Fancy` - receiver annotation target âťŚ
- All the above format correctly to the allowed form âś…

### Key Features

1. **Minimal and targeted**: Only changes the annotation-related logic, existing functionality for other colon uses remains unchanged
2. **Auto-correctable**: The rule can automatically fix the spacing issues
3. **Comprehensive**: Covers all annotation use-site targets mentioned in the issue (`@file:`, `@set:`, `@get:`, `@field:`, `@receiver:`, etc.)
4. **Newline-safe**: Allows newlines in multi-line annotations, only removes regular whitespace characters

The implementation resolves the GitHub issue #2093 that was referenced in the TODO comment.

Perfect! The implementation is complete and correct. Here's a summary of the changes made to resolve the issue:

## Summary of Changes

### Issue
The `SpacingAroundColonRule` previously had a TODO comment and did not enforce spacing rules for colons in annotation use-site targets. This allowed incorrect spacing like `@file :JvmName("Foo")`, `@file: JvmName("Foo")`, and `@file : JvmName("Foo")`.

### Solution

#### 1. Modified `SpacingAroundColonRule.kt` (lines 38-62)

**Key changes:**
- Replaced the early return with active enforcement of no-spacing rule for annotation colons
- Added checks for whitespace before and after the colon (excluding newlines)
- Implemented auto-correction by removing the offending whitespace nodes

**Implementation details:**
```kotlin
val isPartOfAnnotation = node.isPartOf(ANNOTATION) || node.isPartOf(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)

if (isPartOfAnnotation) {
    // Enforce no spacing around colons in annotation use-site targets
    val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
    val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()

    // Check for spacing before the colon
    if (prevLeaf != null && prevLeaf.isWhiteSpace() && !prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
        emit(prevLeaf.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing before \":\" in annotation", true)
        if (autoCorrect) {
            node.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf)
        }
    }

    // Check for spacing after the colon
    if (nextLeaf != null && nextLeaf.isWhiteSpace() && !nextLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
        emit(nextLeaf.startOffset, "Unexpected spacing after \":\" in annotation", true)
        if (autoCorrect) {
            node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf)
        }
    }
    return
}
```

#### 2. Added comprehensive test case in `SpacingAroundColonRuleTest.kt` (lines 105-149)

**Test coverage:**
- Tests all variations of incorrect spacing (before, after, and both sides)
- Validates correct formatting against multiple annotation targets
- Verifies auto-correction functionality
- Ensures no false positives for correctly formatted annotations

### Features
âś… Enforces no spacing around colons in all annotation use-site targets (`@file:`, `@set:`, `@get:`, `@field:`, `@receiver:`, `@property:`, `@param:`, etc.)
âś… Auto-correctable violations
âś… Allows newlines in multi-line annotations  
âś… Minimal, targeted changes that don't affect existing functionality
âś… Comprehensive test coverage

The implementation resolves the GitHub issue #2093 and ensures that spacing is consistently enforced across all annotation targets.

23â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
24â†’
25â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
26â†’
27â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
28â†’
29â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
30â†’
31â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
32â†’
33â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
34â†’
35â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
36â†’
37â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
38â†’
39â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
40â†’
41â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
42â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
43â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
44â†’
45â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
46â†’
47â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
48â†’
49â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
50â†’
